question: What techniques exist for self-improving agents without fine-tuning?

coverage.aspect: limits of self-improvement without external feedback

claim_lines: Self-improving agents without fine-tuning usually fall into three families: (1) **verbal-feedback / reflection loops**, where the agent critiques its own outputs and uses the critique as the next-step signal; (2) **iterative self-refinement**, where the model alternates between generating feedback and revising its answer; and (3) **prompt/workflow optimization**, where the system searches over prompts, plans, or agent workflows instead of updating weights. Reflexion is the canonical example of the first family, using verbal reinforcement learning and reflection to improve agents without costly fine-tuning. Self-Refine is the canonical example of the second. PromptAgent and related prompt-optimization methods show the third family by turning prompt improvement into a search/planning problem.

- **verbal feedback** — covered by Reflexion's verbal reinforcement learning. [Reflexion PDF](https://papers.neurips.cc/paper_files/paper/2023/file/1b44b878bb782e6954cd888628510e90-Paper-Conference.pdf)

- **reflection** — covered by Reflexion's reflection step that converts mistakes into future guidance. [Reflexion PDF](https://papers.neurips.cc/paper_files/paper/2023/file/1b44b878bb782e6954cd888628510e90-Paper-Conference.pdf)

- **self-refinement** — covered by SELF-REFINE's FEEDBACK/REFINE loop without additional training. [Self-Refine PDF](https://papers.nips.cc/paper_files/paper/2023/file/91edff07232fb1b55a505a9e9f6c0ff3-Paper-Conference.pdf)

- **prompt or workflow optimization** — covered by PromptAgent and Automatic Prompt Optimization, both of which search over prompts/workflows rather than fine-tune weights. [PromptAgent](https://arxiv.org/abs/2310.16427), [APO](https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/research/uploads/prod/2023/05/2305.03495.pdf)

- **limits of self-improvement without external feedback** — partially covered: Reflexion motivates its approach by noting that traditional RL needs extensive training samples and expensive fine-tuning, but this is not a proof that self-improvement cannot work without external signals. [Reflexion PDF](https://papers.neurips.cc/paper_files/paper/2023/file/1b44b878bb782e6954cd888628510e90-Paper-Conference.pdf)
unsupported_claim_lines: Self-improving agents without fine-tuning usually fall into three families: (1) **verbal-feedback / reflection loops**, where the agent critiques its own outputs and uses the critique as the next-step signal; (2) **iterative self-refinement**, where the model alternates between generating feedback and revising its answer; and (3) **prompt/workflow optimization**, where the system searches over prompts, plans, or agent workflows instead of updating weights. Reflexion is the canonical example of the first family, using verbal reinforcement learning and reflection to improve agents without costly fine-tuning. Self-Refine is the canonical example of the second. PromptAgent and related prompt-optimization methods show the third family by turning prompt improvement into a search/planning problem.
